one_time_description: Additional ISC COVID-19 funding received for public health measures, income assistance, and reopening costs
economic_notes: COVID-19 impacted First Nations Development Fund revenues and band-owned business revenues. Paul First Nation has multiple business enterprises including Ironhead Golf Course (Wapta Mnodai Developments Ltd.), energy partnerships, industrial closure services, and development corporations. Subsequent to year end, incorporated PFN Land Holdings Inc. for land purchase of $844,000.
notes: Audit is qualified due to inability to obtain sufficient evidence on investments in related First Nation entities (Paul First Nation Industry Relations Corporation and Paul Band Developments Limited Partnership had no audited statements). Trust funds of $11,151,116 are included as financial assets. Operating loan of $954,617 from CMHC is netted in cash equivalents. ISC funding calculated as ISC $20,291,395 plus ISC Health $2,090,653 = $22,382,048. Total government transfers include ISC $22,382,048 plus CMHC $208,860 plus ISETS $617,641 (from segmented schedule) = $23,208,549. CMHC replacement reserve fund is underfunded by $605,605. 8 entities accounted for by modified equity method; 1 fully consolidated subsidiary (Wapta Mnodai Developments Ltd.). No schedule of remuneration included in the financial statements.
